source.section: 4 - AN/ASQ-228 ATFLIR Targeting Pod > 4.5 - Pointing Methods > 4.5.5 - Scene Track & Auto Track

steps.action: Select FLIR page and press the Sensor Control Switch in the direction of the selected DDI.
steps.voiced: Select the FLIR page, then press Sensor Control Switch toward the active DDI.

steps.action: The Select Focus Diamond indicates what display is selected.
steps.voiced: Confirm the Select Focus Diamond appears on the FLIR DDI.

steps.action: Press the Undesignate button twice to select VVSLV mode.
steps.voiced: Press the Undesignate button twice to select VVSLV mode.

steps.action: Press the Undesignate button twice to select unselect VVSLV.
steps.voiced: Press Undesignate twice again to deselect VVSLV.

steps.action: Press the Sensor Control Switch in the direction of the FLIR format page to toggle between Scene Track and Auto Track.
steps.voiced: Press Sensor Control Switch toward the FLIR page to toggle between Scene Track and Auto Track.

steps.action: In Scene Track, the FLIR is slaved to the location of the “scene” (area). However, it the Scene Track reticle can still be slewed with TDC AFT/FWD/LEFT/RIGHT controls (INR SCENE when slewing).
steps.voiced: In Scene Track, FLIR slaves to the scene area — use TDC to slew the reticle; INR SCENE shows when slewing.

steps.action: Target is automatically designated by the targeting pod in Scene Track mode.
steps.voiced: Confirm target is auto-designated in Scene Track mode.

steps.action: Pressing the Undesignate button once will undesignate the target.
steps.voiced: Press Undesignate once to clear the target designation.
